Bob Philips of the American
Eagle at Elk City, Idaho, is in
town, supposedly on a mining deal.


II is rumored that some of the
largest companies of Boundary
Creek district of British Columbia
are to consolidate. but it has not
yet been confirmed.


w. c. Calhoun of Republic was
In the city last night on his way
to Seattle. Mr.. Calhoun is part
owner of the Ruby placer, from
which considerable gold has been
extracted and which is known to
have more than ordinary merit. ut
is not a poor mans proposition, F"
said Mr.. Calhoun. ITO make it
pay big 1t will take company with
money back of it who can afford
to put in dredging machinery and
mine on large scale. My trip to
the coast is not in connection with
the organization or a company,
nevertheless ! shall overlook no OPP
portunities."


The annual meeting of the Molly
Gibson company was held in Mel
son, b. c., yesterday. Colonel S.
w. Ray OF Port Arthur, president
OF the company, states that 1t is
not likely much ore will be shipped
from the property until the price
of silver improves.
